title: Hash-lock RepoBrief semantic extension dependencies
goal: Define supported platforms and create a complete reproducible hash lock plus installation evidence for the optional sentence-transformers and Torch dependency closure without moving semantic reranking into RepoBrief core by default.

acceptance.assertion: Supported Python, operating-system and architecture targets for the semantic extension are explicit; unsupported targets fail closed or remain clearly excluded.
acceptance.verifier: Versioned contract, tests and installation evidence

acceptance.assertion: The full sentence-transformers and Torch transitive closure is exactly versioned and SHA-256 locked for each supported target.
acceptance.verifier: Generated lock files, deterministic regeneration and isolated --require-hashes installs

acceptance.assertion: Semantic dependencies remain optional and do not become an implicit RepoBrief core, snapshot-read or default-ranking dependency.
acceptance.verifier: Diff review, boundary tests and release policy

acceptance.assertion: The evidence does not claim semantic quality, cross-platform completeness, vulnerability absence or default-promotion readiness.

validation: 25 focused semantic-lock and release-packaging tests passed.
The release contract passed under python -S without site packages.
Ruff passed for all changed Python files.
Maintainability ratchet passed with zero new violations and one baseline violation resolved.
The CPython 3.12.3/Linux x86-64 lock reproduced exactly with 58 selected wheel artifacts and unchanged SHA-256 identities.
A real --require-hashes installation into an existing empty target passed; sentence-transformers 5.6.0 and torch 2.13.0+cpu imported offline, CUDA was unavailable and no model was downloaded.

Two deterministic release candidates matched for head 3a1e5897b6c5025532276af2f5cdde7366eee7da: archive SHA-256 c33ffcbdd0fcbb31b06dd6ee33539e7f093c2eb01c6063192fc427824182044a; manifest SHA-256 7b56ce4f30eeab72111e1c708e0926d06d635c1fd46cee3182ee2fc07c6cd1c8.
GitHub PR #1008 checks passed, including pytest-full, semantic-lock, release-candidate, browser-tests, Ruff, CodeQL, contracts, planning-registration and anti-hallucination lint.
Post-merge readback confirmed Lenskit main at db2df91a51354cb6972e197afc7456087a43e70b and the complete merge tree byte-identical to the reviewed PR head.
metadata.outcome: Implemented, critically reviewed, hardened, merged and post-merge verified in Lenskit PR #1008. The optional semantic extension now has one explicit fail-closed CPython 3.12/Linux x86-64/CPU-only platform contract, an exact 58-wheel SHA-256 lock, deterministic regeneration and installation proof, release-candidate binding, non-destructive target handling and a site-package-independent release gate. It remains outside RepoBrief core and disabled by default.

verified_acceptance.rbv1-t019-platform-matrix: The versioned contract supports only CPython 3.12 on Linux x86-64 with CPU-only selected wheels; all other observed targets are rejected fail-closed.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t019-hash-lock: The 58-package sentence-transformers/Torch closure is exactly versioned, bound to selected wheel URLs and one SHA-256 per package, reproducibly regenerated and installed with --require-hashes.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t019-boundary: Core runtime locks contain neither sentence-transformers nor Torch; the release contract and tests require default_enabled=false and prohibit implicit core, snapshot-read or default-ranking dependency status.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t019-non-claims: Proofs and runtime receipts explicitly do not establish semantic quality, cross-platform completeness, vulnerability absence, GPU support, model licensing or readiness for default promotion.
